summary: First iteration: 15/15 backend pytest tests pass (100%); critical frontend flows verified (landing, register link, login with quickfill, dashboard KPIs/chart, all 8 operator nav routes load without redirect, driver portal at /driver loads for driver@acme.com).
action_items: Optional: Add explicit dialog end-to-end Playwright coverage (Create Vehicle/Driver/Customer/Delivery dialogs) - was not exercised in this iteration to save time; pytest covers the underlying APIs end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 745 lines - acceptable for MVP but consider splitting routers (auth/vehicles/drivers/deliveries) into separate modules as features grow (>700 LOC guideline)
Tenant scope filtering is correctly applied across all collections via tenant_scope(user) - good multi-tenant isolation (verified by TestIsolation)
RBAC via require_roles() decorator is consistent and verified for driver/customer denied access
Mongo _id is correctly excluded everywhere using projection {"_id": 0} - good
Public /api/track/{tracking_code} correctly bypasses auth and returns delivery + driver info
test_credentials: owner@acme.com / dispatcher@acme.com / driver@acme.com / customer@acme.com all use Password123!
seed_data_creation: Used existing auto-seeded Acme Logistics org (3 vehicles, 3 drivers, 3 customers, 6 deliveries). Tests create additional TEST_ prefixed entities (vehicle deleted after; customer/driver/delivery left in DB as expected for traceability).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers auth (login/register/me, dup-slug, invalid creds), dashboard summary, vehicle CRUD + RBAC (driver denied), drivers list/create + location update, customers (dispatcher can create, customer role denied /users), full deliveries workflow (create -> assign -> status -> POD -> public track), multi-tenant isolation (fresh org sees empty lists), notifications listing, audit-logs owner-only RBAC. Vehicle model uses registration_number + vehicle_type (NOT plate_number/make/model). Delivery model uses drop_address/drop_lat/drop_lng (NOT delivery_address). Frontend: /app/* protected routes, /driver portal, /portal customer portal, /track/:code public. Login quickfill chips visible on /login.
rca of the issue: N/A - no functional issues found. Two initial pytest failures were test schema mismatches (wrong field names) which were corrected; not product bugs.
